title: Divide whole numbers to get a decimal (2-digit divisors)
description: Dividing larger whole numbers to get a decimal quotient like 63÷12=5.25
topic_path: Module 2: Multi-digit whole number and decimal fraction operations
Topic G: Partial quotients and multi-digit decimal division
Divide whole numbers to get a decimal (2-digit divisors)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$78 \div 12 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
78\div  12&=\dfrac{78}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{72 + 6}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Split 78 into 72 and 6.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{72}{12} + \dfrac{6}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=6 + \dfrac12&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=6 + 0.5&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
78\div  12&=\dfrac{78}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{72 + 6}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Split 78 into 72 and 6.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{72}{12} + \dfrac{6}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=6 + \dfrac12&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=6 + 0.5&\small\gray{\text{Add.}}\\\\
&=6.5
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$63 \div 12 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
63 \div 12&=\dfrac{63}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60 + 3}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Split 63 into 60 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60}{12} + \dfrac{3}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=5 + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=5 + 0.25&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
63 \div 12&=\dfrac{63}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60 + 3}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Split 63 into 60 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{60}{12} + \dfrac{3}{12}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=5 + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=5 + 0.25&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=5.25
\end{align}$

$306 \div 45 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
306 \div 45&=\dfrac{306}{45}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times \dfrac{9}{9}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30 + 4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 34 into 30 and 4.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30}{5} + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
306 \div 45&=\dfrac{306}{45}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times \dfrac{9}{9}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30 + 4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 34 into 30 and 4.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30}{5} + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=6 + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
306 \div 45&=\dfrac{306}{45}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times \dfrac{9}{9}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30 + 4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 34 into 30 and 4.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30}{5} + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=6 + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=6 + 0.8 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
306 \div 45&=\dfrac{306}{45}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times \dfrac{9}{9}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{34}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30 + 4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 34 into 30 and 4.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{30}{5} + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=6 + \dfrac{4}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=6 + 0.8 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=6.8
\end{align}$

$98 \div 56 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
98 \div 56&=\dfrac{98}{56}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{14}{8} \times \dfrac{7}{7}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{7}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 7 into 4 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
98 \div 56&=\dfrac{98}{56}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{14}{8} \times \dfrac{7}{7}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{7}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 7 into 4 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
98 \div 56&=\dfrac{98}{56}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{14}{8} \times \dfrac{7}{7}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{7}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 7 into 4 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=1 + 0.75 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
98 \div 56&=\dfrac{98}{56}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{14}{8} \times \dfrac{7}{7}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{7}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 7 into 4 and 3.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{3}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=1 + 0.75 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=1.75
\end{align}$

$606 \div 15 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
606 \div 15&=\dfrac{606}{15}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times \dfrac{3}{3}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200 + 2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 202 into 200 and 2.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200}{5} + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
606 \div 15&=\dfrac{606}{15}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times \dfrac{3}{3}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200 + 2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 202 into 200 and 2.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200}{5} + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=40 + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
606 \div 15&=\dfrac{606}{15}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times \dfrac{3}{3}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200 + 2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 202 into 200 and 2.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200}{5} + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=40 + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=40 + 0.4 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
606 \div 15&=\dfrac{606}{15}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times \dfrac{3}{3}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{202}{5} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200 + 2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Split 202 into 200 and 2.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{200}{5} + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=40 + \dfrac{2}{5}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=40 + 0.4 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=40.4
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$464 \div 25 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can rewrite the fraction with $100$ in the denominator.

$\begin{align}
464 \div 25&=\dfrac{464}{25}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{464}{25} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{464}{25} \times \dfrac{4}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with equivalent fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1{,}856}{100}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with }100\text{ as denominator.}}\\\\
&=18.56&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$600 \div 80 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
600 \div 80&=\dfrac{600}{80}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{6}{8} \times \dfrac{100}{10}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{4} \times 10&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=0.75 \times 10 &\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=7.5
\end{align}$

$50 \div 40 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
50 \div 40&=\dfrac{50}{40}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times \dfrac{10}{10}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 5 into 4 and 1.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
50 \div 40&=\dfrac{50}{40}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times \dfrac{10}{10}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 5 into 4 and 1.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
50 \div 40&=\dfrac{50}{40}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times \dfrac{10}{10}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 5 into 4 and 1.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=1 + 0.25&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
50 \div 40&=\dfrac{50}{40}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times \dfrac{10}{10}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{4} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4 + 1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Split 5 into 4 and 1.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{4}{4} + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Break into two fractions.}}\\\\
&=1 + \dfrac{1}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
&=1 + 0.25&\small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}\\\\
&=1.25
\end{align}$

$57 \div 75 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
57 \div 75 &= \dfrac{57}{75}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{19}{25} \times \dfrac{3}{3}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{19}{25} \times \dfrac{4}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with equivalent fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{76}{100}& \small\gray{\text{Rewrite with 100 as denominator.}}\\\\
&= 0.76&  \small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$9 \div 45 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
9 \div 45 &= \dfrac{9}{45}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{1}{5} \times \dfrac{9}{9}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{1}{5} \times \dfrac{2}{2}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with equivalent fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2}{10}& \small\gray{\text{Rewrite with 10 as denominator.}}\\\\
&= 0.2&  \small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

**Express your answer as a decimal.**

$7 \div 25 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
7 \div 25 &= \dfrac{7}{25}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{7}{25} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{7}{25} \times \dfrac{4}{4}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with equivalent fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{28}{100}& \small\gray{\text{Rewrite with 100 as denominator.}}\\\\
&= 0.28&  \small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$

$3 \div 50 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Let's rewrite the division problem as a fraction. Then we can split the fraction into better known fractions.

$\begin{align}
3 \div 50 &= \dfrac{3}{50}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite the division as a fraction.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{3}{50} \times 1&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite as a product.}}\\\\
&=  \dfrac{3}{50} \times \dfrac{2}{2}&\small\gray{\text{Rewrite with equivalent fraction.}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{6}{100}& \small\gray{\text{Rewrite with 100 as denominator.}}\\\\
&= 0.06&  \small\gray{\text{Write as a decimal.}}
\end{align}$
